ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. NO OTHER APPEARANCES AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. THE THREE PAGE EXCERPT OF LESLIE HILL'S SEPTEMBER 6, 2018 DEPOSITION IS INSUFFICIENT TO SATISFY DEFENDANT'S BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT CONCLUSIVELY NEGATES AN ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE UNSIGNED DECLARATION OF CRAIG MOUNTZ SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION BECAUSE THE DECLARATION DID NOT COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2015.5. THE COURT ALSO DID NOT CONSIDER DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF ERRATA BECAUSE IT WAS UNTIMELY SERVED UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C(A)(2). FURTHER, THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER DEFENDANT'S REPLY FOR FAILURE TO LODGE COURTESY COPIES WITH THE COURT UNDER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, CLERK: A. MOK. NOT REPORTED.

RULING: DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES
No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. The three page excerpt of Leslie Hill's September 6, 2018 deposition is insufficient to satisfy defendant's burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. The Court did not consider the unsigned declaration of Craig Mountz submitted by defendant in support of the motion because the declaration did not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2015.5. The Court also did not consider defendant's notice of errata because it was untimely served under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2). Further, the Court did not consider defendant's reply for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court under Local Rule 2.7(B). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING: PLAINTIFF LESLIE HILL, SANDRA HILL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is March 4, 2019 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 14, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 28, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 15, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 1, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 14, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
